chunks.title: Benign Lesion Patterns
chunks.text:
Many benign melanocytic nevi are symmetric, evenly pigmented, and stable over time. Stability matters: a lesion that has looked similar for years is generally less concerning than one that has recently changed.

Even when a lesion appears reassuring, assistants should avoid definitive diagnostic language because benign-appearing lesions can still need dermatologist review in the right clinical context.

chunks.title: Benign Lesion Patterns
chunks.text: Seborrheic keratoses are often described as waxy, stuck-on, or verrucous. Their surface texture and sharply demarcated edges can help distinguish them from melanoma, but irritated lesions may still cause confusion and deserve clinician review.
chunks.snippet: Seborrheic keratoses are often described as waxy, stuck-on, or verrucous. Their surface texture and sharply demarcated edges can help distinguish them from melanoma, but irritated lesions may still cause confusion and deserve clinician review.

chunks.title: Benign Lesion Patterns
chunks.text: If a supposedly benign lesion is evolving, symptomatic, or visually unlike surrounding lesions, the system should switch from reassurance to caution. This is especially true when photographs are low quality or classification confidence is weak.
chunks.snippet: If a supposedly benign lesion is evolving, symptomatic, or visually unlike surrounding lesions, the system should switch from reassurance to caution. This is especially true when photographs are low quality or classification confidence is weak.

chunks.title: Biopsy And Follow-Up Context
chunks.text: When a lesion has suspicious ABCDE features, recent change, or the ugly duckling pattern, biopsy is often discussed as the definitive next diagnostic step. An assistant should frame biopsy as something decided by a clinician after in-person assessment rather than implying that every concerning lesion automatically requires the same procedure.

chunks.title: Biopsy And Follow-Up Context
chunks.text: For lower-risk but uncertain lesions, clinicians may use short-interval follow-up, dermoscopy, or serial photography. The safe product behavior is to describe these as examples of dermatologist-led follow-up options rather than care instructions the user should self-manage.
chunks.snippet: For lower-risk but uncertain lesions, clinicians may use short-interval follow-up, dermoscopy, or serial photography. The safe product behavior is to describe these as examples of dermatologist-led follow-up options rather than care instructions the user shoul

chunks.title: Dermatology Triage And Safety
chunks.text: A dermatology assistant should not attempt routine self-care guidance when a user describes severe infection, rapidly spreading rash with systemic symptoms, angioedema, breathing difficulty, mucosal blistering, or signs of sepsis. These cases should be redirected toward urgent medical evaluation.
chunks.snippet: A dermatology assistant should not attempt routine self-care guidance when a user describes severe infection, rapidly spreading rash with systemic symptoms, angioedema, breathing difficulty, mucosal blistering, or signs of sepsis. These cases should be redirec

chunks.title: Dermatology Triage And Safety
chunks.text: When retrieval support is thin or the classifier confidence is low, the assistant should say so clearly. The correct behavior is to explain uncertainty, cite what evidence is available, and recommend clinician follow-up rather than producing confident diagnostic claims.
chunks.snippet: When retrieval support is thin or the classifier confidence is low, the assistant should say so clearly. The correct behavior is to explain uncertainty, cite what evidence is available, and recommend clinician follow-up rather than producing confident diagnost

chunks.title: Dermatology Triage And Safety
chunks.text: The system should consistently remind users that it is an informational tool, not a substitute for a board-certified dermatologist. Safety language should be direct and calm, not alarmist.
chunks.snippet: The system should consistently remind users that it is an informational tool, not a substitute for a board-certified dermatologist. Safety language should be direct and calm, not alarmist.

chunks.title: Melanoma Overview
chunks.text: Melanoma is a type of skin cancer that arises from pigment-producing melanocytes. It is clinically important because it can become invasive and spread if it is not recognized and evaluated early.
chunks.snippet: Melanoma is a type of skin cancer that arises from pigment-producing melanocytes. It is clinically important because it can become invasive and spread if it is not recognized and evaluated early.

chunks.title: Melanoma Overview
chunks.text: Melanoma discussions usually focus on early recognition, lesion change over time, and prompt dermatologist assessment for suspicious lesions. In a triage setting, the product should explain the basics clearly without implying that every dark lesion is melanoma.
chunks.snippet: Melanoma discussions usually focus on early recognition, lesion change over time, and prompt dermatologist assessment for suspicious lesions. In a triage setting, the product should explain the basics clearly without implying that every dark lesion is melanoma

chunks.title: Melanoma Overview
chunks.text: When a user asks broad questions such as "what is melanoma," the assistant should begin with a simple definition, then describe common warning patterns and the need for professional evaluation when a lesion is changing, bleeding, painful, or otherwise concerning.
chunks.snippet: When a user asks broad questions such as "what is melanoma," the assistant should begin with a simple definition, then describe common warning patterns and the need for professional evaluation when a lesion is changing, bleeding, painful, or otherwise concerni

chunks.title: Melanoma Warning Signs
chunks.text:
The ABCDE framework is commonly used to flag lesions that deserve prompt dermatologist review. Clinicians look for asymmetry, irregular borders, multiple colors, diameter growth, and recent evolution in shape, size, color, or symptoms.

Evolution is especially important because a lesion that is changing over weeks to months may be more concerning than a stable lesion with otherwise mild atypia. Symptoms such as bleeding, itching, ulceration, or persistent crusting should increase caution rather than reassure.
chunks.snippet: The ABCDE framework is commonly used to flag lesions that deserve prompt dermatologist review. Clinicians look for asymmetry, irregular borders, multiple colors, diameter growth, and recent evolution in shape, size, color, or symptoms.  Evolution is especially

chunks.title: Melanoma Warning Signs
chunks.text:
Melanoma concern is higher when a patient reports a personal history of melanoma, strong family history, intense ultraviolet exposure, or a lesion that looks different from the patient's other moles. This "ugly duckling" pattern is a practical signal in triage conversations.

Any lesion associated with rapid growth, spontaneous bleeding, pain, or systemic symptoms should trigger escalation instead of casual reassurance. A cautious assistant should encourage urgent in-person assessment when these patterns are described.
chunks.snippet: Melanoma concern is higher when a patient reports a personal history of melanoma, strong family history, intense ultraviolet exposure, or a lesion that looks different from the patient's other moles. This "ugly duckling" pattern is a practical signal in triage
